# Russia Will Never Agree to Peace in Ukraine, Reshaping NATO and European Defense Strategy

## Overview
An analysis argues Vladimir Putin's regime requires perpetual war in Ukraine, fundamentally altering NATO strategy and European defense approaches. It highlights structural incentives for ongoing conflict that preclude negotiated peace. This has direct implications for European security amid tensions with Russia.

## Details
The article posits that Putin's authoritarian system depends on continuous warfare in Ukraine to maintain domestic control and external aggression, making genuine peace impossible. This reality demands a complete overhaul of NATO's deterrence posture and European defense spending priorities. It warns that assumptions of eventual Russian compromise have weakened alliance preparedness. Analysts emphasize shifting from de-escalation fantasies to robust long-term containment, including enhanced forward deployments. The piece connects this to broader European security architecture, urging unity against hybrid threats from Moscow. No direct China link, but frames Russia as the primary disruptor.[2]

## Location
Ukraine/Europe

## Source Context
Reporting attributed to coverage tracked under @cepa (CEPA). Primary citation provides authoritative public reporting on this development. Cross-source verification through Perplexity sonar-indexed news outlets reinforces the account.
